session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8869 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load and follow the skill instructions to classify PR feedback for PR 8869.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll fetch the PR feedback for PR 8869 and the file-level restructuring context:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Show file restructuring context with renames and copies

session.tool_result:
{ "success": true, "pr_number": 8869, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8869", "pr_title": "Consolidate documentation from 9 erk-learn plans", "review_threads": [ { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5ytCxs", "path": "docs/learned/architecture/health-check-runner-gateway.md", "line": 27, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: "`HealthCheckRunner` ABC with single method: `run_all(ctx, *, check_hooks: bool) -> list[CheckResult]`" is described as being in `src/erk/core/health_checks/runner.py`

Reality: The `HealthCheckRunner` ABC is defined in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/health_check_runner.py`. The file `src/erk/core/health_checks/runner.py` only contains `RealHealthCheckRunner` (which imports and subclasses the ABC).

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/health_check_runner.py`

Suggested fix: Update the **File:** path to `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/health_check_runner.py` for the ABC, and clarify that `src/erk/core/health_checks/runner.py` contains only the real implementation.", "created_at": "2026-03-06T23:08:11Z" } ] }, { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5ytC2D", "path": "docs/learned/architecture/progress-feedback-threading.md", "line": 31, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — documents private constant name that will change.

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/shared.py` (line 342) and `src/erk/core/commit_message_generator.py`

Risk: Private constant names like `_PROGRESS_INTERVAL_SECONDS` and `_PROGRESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` are implementation details that can be renamed without notice. Consider replacing with the numeric values in context (`2.0s` and `5.0s`) or removing the constant name references and keeping only the conceptual description.", "created_at": "2026-03-06T23:08:21Z" }

session.assistant:
Perfect. Now I have all the information needed. Let me classify the PR feedback according to the skill instructions:
**Analysis:**
PR 8869 has 4 unresolved review threads, all from the `github-actions` bot (not human). The PR involves file additions (consolidating documentation from 9 erk-learn plans), not file restructuring (renames/moves). The `git diff --stat` shows new files being added to `docs/learned/`, not moving/renaming existing files.
**Thread Classifications:**
1. **health-check-runner-gateway.md:27** - Inaccurate claim about ABC location. This is a human-detectable error in documentation content, not a pre-existing code quality issue. Pre-existing: false.
2. **progress-feedback-threading.md:31** - Documents private constant names that may change. This is drift risk. Not pre-existing because there's no file restructuring involved. Pre-existing: false.
3. **pr-prepare.md:22** - Documents specific line count that will change. Drift risk. Not pre-existing. Pre-existing: false.
4. **reconcile.md:27** - Documents specific file line counts. Drift risk. Not pre-existing. Pre-existing: false.
All threads are actionable (bot suggestions to improve documentation), single-file changes, no pre-existing issues detected.

specific counts become misleading as files evolve", "complexity": "single_file", "original_comment": "Audit PR Docs: Drift risk — documents specific file line counts that will change.\n\nSource: `src/erk/cli/commands/reconcile_cmd.py` and `src/erk/cli/commands/reconcile_pipeline.py`" } ], "discussion_actions": [], "informational_count": 3, "batches": [ { "name": "Pre-Existing (Auto-Resolve)", "complexity": "pre_existing", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Local Fixes", "complexity": "local", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Single-File", "complexity": "single_file", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [0, 1, 2, 3] }, { "name": "Cross-Cutting", "complexity": "cross_cutting", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Complex", "complexity": "complex", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Informational", "complexity": "informational", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] } ], "error": null } ```
